_patch_meta.description: C17 Dimension Review — Part 2: Reg 62 (fellowship, 4) + Reg 64 (forgiveness, 14) + Reg 99 (kindness, 28) + Phase B.5 rewrites (2 groups)

set.context_description: Spiritual faintness and trust — the inner person in extremity, directing its cry and its trust toward God as the one who knows the way
set.notes: Revised during Dimension Review Phase B.5: characteristic-perspective rewrite. Old: Term connects and qualifies inner-being content — directing attention to persons or conditions where inner life is expressed

set.context_description: Spiritual faintness and trust — the inner person in extremity, directing its cry and its trust toward God as the one who knows the way

operations.description: Group 5769-001: sync context_description after Phase B.5 rewrite

set.context_description: Persistence and turning — the duration of inner moral orientation, whether integrity maintained under affliction or stubbornness that must cease
set.notes: Revised during Dimension Review Phase B.5: characteristic-perspective rewrite. Old: Term intensifies or marks the persistence/cessation of a human inner-being state — amplifying the force of an inner disposition or qualifying the duration of inner orientation

set.context_description: Persistence and turning — the duration of inner moral orientation, whether integrity maintained under affliction or stubbornness that must cease

operations.description: Group 990-001: sync context_description after Phase B.5 rewrite

set.notes: The inner-being reality of participating in divine nature — the human person constituted as a sharer in what belongs to God. This is Divine-Human Correspondence: the characteristic is defined by the crossing of the divine-human boundary, with the human as the one who participates in what is originally divine. Automated label confirmed. DOMINANT-SUBJECT: HUMAN — it is the human person who becomes a

The inner orientation that constitutes moral solidarity or complicity — whether one is inwardly aligned with a person or cause. Automated label Moral Character confirmed. The group captures koinōnos in its moral-character dimension: the inner quality of the person that constitutes them a participant in another's moral acts.

The inner-relational orientation of communion with the divine persons — the human person's participation in the shared life of the Father and Son. This is Divine-Human Correspondence: the defining feature of the group is that fellowship crosses the divine-human boundary; the human participates in what is intrinsically divine (the Father-Son relationship). The automated label Relational Disposition

The horizontal inner-relational bond among believers — mutual belonging and shared life grounded in shared inner alignment. The automated label Volition misses the relational orientation dimension — this is not primarily a choice or act of will but an inner-relational bond. Correcting from Volition to Relational Disposition.

The deliberate inner act of choosing to leave — the will reordered around Christ expressed through forsaking prior commitments. This is Volition: the inner act of purposing and choosing expressed outwardly. The UNCLASSIFIED label corrected to Volition.

The abandonment of relational commitment — the inner failure of loyalty expressed as leaving. Automated label Relational Disposition confirmed: the characteristic is the relational orientation (or its withdrawal).
The relational priority expressed through the leave/stay choice — the inner orientation that places relational restoration above other obligations. Automated label Relational Disposition confirmed.

God's act of releasing the person from guilt — the vertical forgiveness that restores the inner standing and produces love. The automated label Divine-Human Correspondence is present, but Luk 7:47 does not show a mirroring structure; it shows a causal chain from divine act to human inner state. Correcting to Relational Disposition, GOD. The divine action is the dominant subject — it is God who for

The human act of forgiving — releasing another from the inner claim of the offended — "from the heart." This is Relational Disposition: the inner orientation of the forgiver toward the offender expressed as release. The automated label Divine-Human Correspondence is present (implied by the "so also" framing), but the verse foregrounds the human act. Correcting to Relational Disposition, HUMAN.

Permitting access as the expression of an inner orientation of welcome or priority — the inner relational stance that determines who is let in. With 12 verses the corpus spans both divine (Jesus permitting/welcoming) and human (disciples hindering/permitting) subjects. Dominant-subject NONE — both poles are represented across the corpus. Automated label Volition is partially accurate (the act of d

The withholding of permission as an expression of the actor's inner moral condition — hypocritical obstruction that flows from a closed inner orientation. The automated label Agency/Power captures the exercise of authority dimension but misses the moral-character content: the act reveals the inner state of the person. Correcting to Moral Character.

God's act of forgiving sin as the outcome of atonement — the divine disposition of forgiveness expressed through the cultic mechanism. This is God's relational orientation of mercy enacted through priestly intercession. The automated label Agency/Power names the exercise of divine authority but misses the relational-disposition quality: the forgiveness is an expression of divine mercy and the rest

The act of seeking divine forgiveness through intercession — the earnest plea from a posture of creaturely dependence and urgent need. The automated label Divine-Human Correspondence is present (the plea and God's response form a dialogue), but the description focuses on the human act of intercession rather than a correspondence structure. The dominant inner-being characteristic is the human postu

God's unconditional new covenant forgiveness — the divine inner act of releasing iniquity and choosing not to remember sin. This is a supreme expression of divine relational disposition: the sustained, unconditional orientation of mercy that defines the new covenant relationship. The automated label Divine-Human Correspondence is present but Jer 31:34 does not show a mirroring structure — it shows

Forgiveness as a defining inner attribute of God — the settled disposition of readiness to pardon that belongs to his essential character. This is the most concentrated expression of divine relational disposition in the forgiveness vocabulary: sal.lach names forgiveness not as an act but as a character quality. The automated label Divine-Human Correspondence is not appropriate (no mirroring struct

Forgiveness of sins through Christ's blood — the divine act of releasing the inner person from guilt through grace. God's relational disposition of forgiveness expressed through the redemptive act. The UNCLASSIFIED label corrected to Relational Disposition, GOD.

The permanent inner state of unforgiven guilt — the condition of the person for whom forgiveness is inaccessible. This is the darkest expression in the forgiveness vocabulary: the inner-being condition of permanent estrangement from divine pardon. Emotion — Negative captures the inner state dimension (permanent guilt, estrangement). The UNCLASSIFIED label corrected to Emotion — Negative.

Forgiveness as a divine attribute that grounds the human-divine relationship — the divine character quality that makes approach and fear simultaneously possible. The automated label Emotion — Negative was flagged in Phase B as misassigned (likely triggered by "feared"). The inner characteristic is God's relational disposition of forgiveness, not the human emotional state of fear. Correcting to Rel
Kindness as the active expression of love's inner character toward others — a relational orientation expressed outwardly. Automated label Relational Disposition confirmed.

The deceptive inner disposition that produces flattering speech — self-serving appetite masquerading as kindness. This is a stable inner character disposition (Moral Character), not primarily a relational orientation toward another. The automated label Relational Disposition (ROOT_INFERRED) misses the moral-character dimension: the characteristic is the inner condition of deceptive self-interest.

The inner states of grief, guilt, and fear of separation expressed in the context of departure from relational presence. The dominant characteristic across both anchors is the negative inner emotional experience triggered by or feared in departure. Automated label Emotion — Negative (KEYWORD_STRONG) confirmed. The preposition itself (me.im) is the property term; the inner-being characteristic is t

Fear and its overcoming as the communal inner response in the context of war. The inner-being characteristic is the emotional orientation of the military community — fear (its presence, its command against, its replacement by trust). Automated label Emotion — Negative confirmed.

The inner-being dimension of communal belonging — the orientation of inclusion or exclusion from the covenant community. Automated label Volition misses the relational-orientation quality; this is about the inner belonging-bond, not primarily about choosing. Correcting to Relational Disposition.

The relational presence of God as the condition of the human creature's inner restoration, petition, and orientation toward God. Both anchors show the human person in extreme dependence — facing death (Dan 2) or restored from degradation (Dan 4) — with divine presence as the critical context. Dependence/Creatureliness more precisely captures the inner posture than the automated Relational Disposit

Moses's inner orientation of solidarity and advocacy for the covenant people — a relational disposition of generosity and intercession toward the community, grounded in theological conviction. The automated label Moral Character named only the community as moral category; the researcher-confirmed description refocuses on Moses's inner posture. Relational Disposition is the correct dimension: Moses

The corporate inner spiritual condition of rebellion, unbelief, and despising — stable inner dispositions of the community that constitute its moral-spiritual character before God. Automated label Emotion — Negative is partially accurate (fear is present) but misses the dominant characteristic: the rebellion and unbelief are moral-character qualities, not merely emotional states. Correcting to Mor

Pharaoh's hardened refusal as the inner obstacle to the people's liberation — the volitional inner state of refusal/hardness is the primary inner-being content. The researcher-confirmed description correctly identifies this. Volition (the inner act of refusing/hardening the will) is the appropriate dimension. The automated label Moral Character named the people's identity; the corrected descriptio
Identical to 5737-001. Moses's inner orientation of solidarity and advocacy.
Identical to 5737-002. Corporate inner spiritual condition of rebellion and unbelief.
Identical to 5737-003. Pharaoh's hardened refusal as inner volitional obstacle.
Identical to 5737-001. Moses's inner orientation of solidarity and advocacy.
Identical to 5737-002. Corporate inner spiritual condition of rebellion and unbelief.
Identical to 5737-003. Pharaoh's hardened refusal as inner volitional obstacle.

Spiritual faintness and trust in extremity — the inner person at the limit of its own resources, directing itself toward God. This is Dependence/Creatureliness: the creature at the end of itself, relying entirely on God's knowledge and love. Automated label Vitality/Existence is close (the fainting spirit touches on vitality/existence of the inner person) but Dependence/Creatureliness more precise

The inner act of reflective recognition — Qoheleth's wisdom-seeing that identifies enjoyment and acceptance as gifts of God. This is a cognitive inner act (recognising, seeing, knowing) directed toward the meaning of human experience. Automated label Emotion — Positive (KEYWORD_WEAK) misses the reflective-wisdom quality: the group is not primarily about positive inner feeling but about the inner a

Self-glorifying pride as an inner character disposition — the settled orientation of self-exaltation that Dan 4 will expose and humble. The UNCLASSIFIED label corrected to Moral Character: pride/self-glorification is a stable inner character orientation.

The inner moral orientation (faithfulness or infidelity) whose consequences are named — the particle points to the moral cause and its outcome. Moral Character is confirmed: the group is about stable inner dispositions (covenant-keeping vs. spiritual infidelity) and their consequences. Automated label Moral Character confirmed.

The particle zeh functions as an inner-being identifier — it points to and specifies the object of divine attention or the act of inner recognition. The inner-being characteristic the particle frames across both anchors is: (Isa 58:6) the inner-oriented fast of justice, and (Isa 66:2) the inner character of humility and contrition. Both are about recognising and naming what is genuinely inner befo

Divine presence as the condition of the human creature's inner trust and security — the person's inner orientation is entirely dependent on divine accompaniment. Automated label Dependence/Creatureliness confirmed. Psa 23:4 is a paradigmatic anchor for this dimension.

The inner state of spiritual devastation and self-diminishment in the zone of direct divine encounter experienced as afflictive. The automated label Divine-Human Correspondence is present but these verses do not show mirroring — they show the human inner person overwhelmed by divine action. Correcting to Emotion — Negative: the inner states (spiritual poisoning, fearful self-diminishment) are nega

The inner orientation of covenant faithfulness and loyalty within the relational bond — the toward-another disposition expressed through steadfast love and loyal action. Automated label Moral Character is close but misses the relational orientation: the characteristic is directed toward another (Jonathan toward David, David toward Hanun), not merely a stable quality of character. Correcting to Rel
The evil inclination of the heart as the defining inner moral condition of fallen humanity — a stable inner disposition toward wickedness. Automated label Moral Character confirmed.
Human frailty and creaturely dependence as the defining inner orientation of adam — the creature's proper acknowledgment of limitation before God. Automated label Dependence/Creatureliness confirmed.

Humanity constituted in the divine image, called to reflect divine character — this is precisely Divine-Human Correspondence: the inner-being of the human person is defined by structural correspondence with God (image/likeness) and by the requirement to embody the character qualities that reflect God (justice, kindness, humility). The automated label Volition misses the constitutive correspondence

Meekness, wisdom, contrition, and humility as representative inner dispositions of individual human persons — stable character qualities that define the person. Automated label Cognition misses the character-quality dimension; knowing and perceiving are not the primary content. Correcting to Moral Character.

Goodness/kindness as the inner quality of gracious favour toward others — a relational disposition in both God (Rom 2:4) and the human person (Eph 4:32). The group spans both divine and human subjects across the 6-verse corpus. Dominant-subject NONE — genuinely dual. Automated label Moral Character is close but misses the relational orientation quality; the chrēstos virtue is fundamentally directe

_patch_summary.note: No registry stamps — applied in Part 4
